info.name: Path of the World Tree
info.description: The Path of the World Tree subclass from Jay's Guide to Maa.
info.author: Jay Ahuja

p.em: Trace the Roots and Branches of the Multiverse

description.p: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree believe that their Rage links them to the cosmic ash tree Yggdrasil. This "world tree" grows among the Outer Planes, connecting them to each other and to the many worlds of the Material Plane, and the greatest plants on every world are said to be distant descendants of mighty Yggdrasil. These Barbarians draw on their connection to the world tree as a source of vitality and as a means of travel across the multiverse.

sheet.description: Barbarians who follow the Path of the World Tree believe that their Rage links them to the cosmic ash tree Yggdrasil.

description.p: Your Rage taps into the lifeforce of the World Tree. You gain the following benefits:
Vitality Surge. When you activate your Rage, you gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your Barbarian level.

Life-Giving Force. At the start of each of your turns while your Rage is active, you can choose another creature within 10 feet of yourself to gain Temporary Hit Points. To determine the number of Temporary Hit Points, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. If any of these Temporary Hit Points remain when your Rage ends, they vanish.

sheet.description: When you activate Rage, gain {{level:barbarian}} Temporary HP. At the start of each turn while Raging, grant {{barbarian rage:damage}}d6 Temporary HP to a creature within 10 feet.

description.p: While your Rage is active, whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to summon spectral branches of the World Tree around it. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equal to 8 plus your Proficiency Bonus plus your Strength modifier) or be teleported to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of yourself or in the nearest unoccupied space you can see. The space the target teleports to must be on a surface or liquid that can support it; otherwise, the target doesn't teleport.
After the target teleports, you can reduce its Speed to 0 until the end of the current turn.

sheet.description: While Raging, whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you, use Reaction to teleport them within 5 feet of you on failed DC {{branches of the tree:dc}} Strength save, and reduce their Speed to 0.

description.p: Tendrils of the World Tree extend from your Melee weapons. During your turn, while you wield a Melee weapon with the Heavy or Versatile property, your reach with that weapon increases by 10 feet. Additionally once per turn, when you hit with this weapon, you may force the target to make a constitution save (DC 8+Your Strength Modifier+Your Proficiency Bonus) on fail, you may choose to push the target 10 feet or fall prone.

sheet.description: While wielding Heavy or Versatile melee weapons on your turn, your reach increases by 10 feet. Additionally, once per turn, on a hit with this weapon, force the target to make a constitution save (DC{{batteringboots}}). On fail, you may push them 10 feet or make them fall prone.

description.p: You can briefly travel along the World Tree. When you activate your Rage and as a Bonus Action while it's active, you can teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

In addition, when you teleport using this feature, you can bring up to six willing creatures who are within 10 feet of yourself, and the range you can teleport increases to 500 feet. Each creature teleports to an unoccupied space of your choice within 10 feet of your destination space. You can teleport other creatures with you only once per Rage.

sheet.description: When you activate Rage or as a Bonus Action while Raging, teleport up to 60 feet. Also once per Rage can bring up to 6 willing creatures within 10 feet, increasing range to 500 feet.

element.name: Vitality of the Tree Feature Replacement
Branches of the Tree Feature Replacement
Travel along the Tree Feature Replacement
